Functions for delaying execution at microsecond and millisecond granularity.

# Purpose
This header file declares two functions, [`delay_us`](<#delay_us>) and [`delay_ms`](<#delay_ms>), which provide delay functionality at microsecond and millisecond granularity, respectively. The [`delay_us`](<#delay_us>) function is specifically noted to use a carefully timed spin loop to achieve precise microsecond delays. Both functions take a single parameter of type `uint32_t`, representing the number of microseconds or milliseconds to delay. The file includes the `<inttypes.h>` header to ensure the availability of fixed-width integer types. The use of `#pragma once` prevents multiple inclusions of this header file in a single compilation unit.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `inttypes.h`
